title: Pride Month Demon
about:
Pride Month Demon, also known as Pride Demon or Pride Month Demon Shirt, is a word-art graphic that repeats the phrase "Pride Month" five times, narrowing down the letters to the word "Demon" written in rainbow colors. The design was originally created by Sword-In-Hand Publishing in 2021, perceived as anti-LGBTQ+. Soon after, in 2021, queer Redbubble artist Art by Veya (artbyveya) created the rainbow-colored iteration for pro-LGBTQ+ usage.

In late May 2023, just before Pride Month 2023, Art by Veya's graphic went viral on Twitter when GOP politician Lauren Witzke tweeted it. Thereafter, many parodied the graphic or humorously imagined what a "Pride Month Demon" was in memes.
origin:
Sometime before June 24th, 2021, the since-deleted Facebook[1] page of Sword-In-Hand Publishing posted an infographic that narrowed down the phrase "Pride Month" to "Demon" written in red (shown below, left). On June 24th, 2021, the first currently known sharing of a Facebook screenshot was posted on Reddit [2] (shown below, right).

On June 26th, 2021, Twitter[3] user @thedabqueenS tweeted the infographic, writing, "Love how this was posted unironically 🙃😂," gaining over 160 likes in two years (shown below, left). A month later, on July 18th, 2021, Twitter[4] user @binej_yeah captioned it, "this image is so fucking funny," gaining roughly 17,000 likes in two years (shown below, right).

Around the same time in 2021, Redbubble[5] artist artbyveya started their shop, which mostly included LGBTQ+ Pride Month merch that showed a variant of the "Pride Month" to "Demon" graphic using the colors of the Pride flag and Transgender flag (examples shown below). Redbubble's artbyveya proclaimed in their profile[6] that they were "a queer, non-binary artist" with a "passion for human rights, the LGBTQIA+ community and all things snarky." Their identity as a queer artist is also confirmed by their Instagram,[7] ultimately likening the shirt's design to a parody of Sword-In-Hand Publishing's design. The design would then become the subject of memes two years later on Twitter in 2023.
